title: Olleco Opens New UCO-Processing Plant in Liverpool
content:
(Olleco/Biobased Diesel Daily) U.K.-based Olleco announced in late February the opening of its new used cooking oil (UCO) processing plant in Liverpool. The UCO collected by Olleco will be refined in this new facility and then sent through a pipe to its biodiesel plant.

In addition to a comprehensive network of local depots, Ollecoâ€™s website states the company operates three UCO biorefineries, a biodiesel plant, a bulk liquid-storage facility and three anaerobic-digestion (AD) plants with support from a wider network of licensed AD facilities.

The biodiesel produced delivers carbon savings of 89 percent compared to fossil diesel.

The biodiesel will be dispensed at fueling stations and used in transport fleets.
author: Analysts Account
